$schema: internal — payer-by-payer bariatric surgery prior auth criteria
verificationNotes: Every entry verified live by research subagent on 2026-04-07 against the cited source URL. Re-verify quarterly per the staleness audit recommendation.

payers.label: NIH 1991 Consensus (academic standard)
payers.notes: Foundational 1991 NIH Consensus Development Conference statement; still the academic baseline cited by Medicare and most commercial insurers.

payers.label: ASMBS / IFSO 2022 (current academic standard)
payers.notes: Eisenberg et al. 2022 ASMBS/IFSO indications statement (PMID 36280539). Recommends MBS at BMI ≥35 regardless of comorbidities and at BMI 30-34.9 with metabolic disease. Most US payers have not yet adopted these lower thresholds.

payers.label: Medicare NCD 100.1
payers.facilityRequirement: ASMBS Center of Excellence or MBSAQIP-accredited
payers.notes: Medicare NCD 100.1 requires BMI ≥35 + at least one obesity-related comorbidity + prior failure of medical weight management. Covers RYGB, BPD/DS, sleeve gastrectomy, LAGB. Vertical-banded gastroplasty not covered.

payers.label: Aetna CPB 0157
payers.notes: Aetna requires 12 supervised diet sessions on separate dates within 2 years (≈6 months elapsed). 2-year documented duration of obesity. Adolescents (post-bone-growth) ≥40 BMI eligible.

payers.label: Cigna Coverage Policy 0051
payers.notes: Cigna requires preauthorization, documented prior failed weight loss attempts, psychological evaluation, nutritional counseling, and letters of medical necessity.

payers.label: UnitedHealthcare
payers.notes: UnitedHealthcare requires detailed weight/dietary/exercise history and psychosocial-behavioral evaluation. Plan variation across UHC products.

payers.label: Blue Cross Blue Shield (FEP reference)
payers.notes: FEP medical policy 7.01.47. State BCBS licensees vary; this is the federal employee program reference. Includes a Class 1 obesity + T2D pathway at BMI 30-34.9.

payers.label: Humana
payers.notes: Humana requires a 6-month physician-supervised weight loss program, multidisciplinary surgical preparatory regimen, psychological evaluation, and PCP letter of recommendation.
